Project.name: kompakt
Project.annotation: Free, private, offline PDF compression desktop CLI with dual-engine compression (Ghostscript + pypdf)
Project.keywords: pdf-compression, cli, ghostscript, pypdf, python, desktop, terminal, offline, open-source

Actors.actor-User: Developer, designer, or office worker who needs to compress PDFs via terminal
Actors.actor-CI: GitHub Actions runner that builds binaries and publishes releases

UC-001.Goal: Interactively select a PDF file and compression quality
UC-001.Preconditions: At least one PDF exists in current directory
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: User sees fuzzy search for file, quality selector, and before/after stats

UC-002.Goal: Non-interactive compression with specified quality level
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: Compressed output PDF is produced with correct DPI

UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: Shows which engines are available and how to install missing ones

UC-004.Goal: Build and release binaries for all three platforms
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: GitHub Release contains binaries for macOS, Linux, Windows

NonGoals.item-1: No cloud processing or server-side compression.
NonGoals.item-3: No PDF editing, merging, or splitting features.

Constraints.constraint-1: Must work on macOS, Linux, and Windows.
Constraints.constraint-2: Must function with zero dependencies when Ghostscript and pypdf are absent (graceful degradation).
Constraints.constraint-3: No network requests during compression — fully offline.

Risks.risk-1: Ghostscript availability and path resolution varies across platforms.
Risks.risk-2: PDF files with complex fonts or embedded objects may corrupt under aggressive compression.
Risks.risk-3: PyInstaller binary size and startup time on Windows.

OpenQuestions.question-1: Should batch processing be added as a separate CLI flag?
OpenQuestions.question-2: Should compression presets include custom DPI values?
